title: media.rs: process_remote buffers full response body before size check
description: resp.bytes().await buffers the entire HTTP response body into memory before checking against MAX_FILE_SIZE. A malicious or misbehaving remote server can send a multi-gigabyte body, potentially exhausting memory. Fix: check Content-Length header first and reject if it exceeds MAX_FILE_SIZE, then stream with a byte-count limit (e.g. read chunks and abort if total exceeds limit). The current approach also hides this from the reqwest timeout, which is a duration timeout not a size timeout.
close_reason: All pass 4 fixes applied: media SSRF+size guard, mime/format consistency, proper keyId parsing, fail-closed sig verify, path reconstruction, key owner validation, media cap, blocking stdin fix, conflicts_with
